title: Method contracts and parameters deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Method contracts and parameters; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Method contracts and parameters: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Java passes objects by reference, so assigning a new object to a parameter also changes the caller's variable.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Java is pass-by-value: a reference value is copied for an object argument, so parameter reassignment stays local while mutation through the shared reference remains observable.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Java is pass-by-value: a reference value is copied for an object argument, so parameter reassignment stays local while mutation through the shared reference remains observable.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Java is pass-by-value: a reference value is copied for an object argument, so parameter reassignment stays local while mutation through the shared reference remains observable.
Java passes objects by reference, so assigning a new object to a parameter also changes the caller's variable.

answer.explanation: Java is pass-by-value: a reference value is copied for an object argument, so parameter reassignment stays local while mutation through the shared reference remains observable.

items.title: Method contracts and parameters: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read value, minimum, and maximum integers. Implement and call a clamp method that returns the inclusive bounded result without mutation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Java: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The static method has one typed purpose, returns its result, and has no hidden effect on caller state.
